title: practical english usage - Part 77
content:
For infinitives or infinitive clauses as the objects of verbs, ►    97.

It was my ambition to retire at thirty.

It is your task to get across the river without being seen.

Sentences like these can also be constructed with preparatory it (►  268).

grammar •  94  -ing form as subject, object or complement not I saw him  getting out of the car. (     I saw his getting...) with -ing forms, in formal or informal language.

After some verbs (e.g. see, hear, watch, feel) possessives are not normally used She was angry at Lina lying to her.

Do you mind me smoking?

after a verb or preposition.

instead of possessives (my, Jack's) with -ing forms, especially when these come In an informal style it is more common to use object forms (like me, Jack) 4 object     forms:    Do   you     mind      me    smoking?

She's made up her mind; there's no arguing with her.

Sorry - there's no smoking in the waiting room.

NO  SMOKING   NO  PARKING  NO  WAITING impossible. This often happens in notices and after there is.

No is often used with an -ing form to say that something is not allowed, or is not the smoking of cigarettes (     the smoking cigarettes) Instead, we  can use an o/-strueture.

When an -ing form is used with an article, it cannot usually have a direct object.

Thank you for waiting, (     Thanh you foryour waiting.) already clear who  is being talked about.

Note that possessives and pronouns are not used before -ing forms if it is but not His shouting gets on my nerves, (          He shouting...) Subject pronouns are not possible.

She was angry at Lina's trying to lie to her.

Jack's going to sleep during the wedding was rather embarrassing.

Possessive 's forms are also possible.

I hate all this useless arguing.

the opening of Parliament Does my smoking annoy you?

We   can often use determiners (for example the, my, this) with -ing forms.

3 the    opening         of   Parliament;           my    smoking I hate packing suitcases.

Smoking cigarettes is bad for you.  My favourite activity is reading thrillers.

verb and can have its own object.

The  -ing form subject, complement or object is used like a noun, but it is still a 2 -ing    form    with    its  own    object 1 hate packing, (object) -ing forms can also be used as objects after certain verbs (►   100).

formal and uncommon.

Infinitives (e.g. To smoke is bad for you) are possible in these cases, but are My favourite activity is reading, (complement) Smoking is bad for you. (subject) complement of a verb.

An -ing form (gerund) can be used, just like a noun, as the subject or 1 Smoking          is  bad     for    you.

her brother, I was sorry not to meet him.) when it's true, but there are too many exceptions. (Compare I anticipated seeing I enjoyed meeting her parents). Unfortunately, this is not a reliable rule: it's true is forward in time, and -ing forms in other cases (as in I expected to see Megan; Students’  grammars sometimes say that infinitives are used when the reference A  bad rule activity is to read thrillers.) My favourite activity is reading thrillers. (More natural than My favourite kill you.) Smoking cigarettes can kill you. (More natural than To smoke cigarettes can English. Compare: complements after be. Infinitives are less common, especially in informal Expressions with -ing forms can be used both as subjects in sentences, or as check  in a good dictionary.

are followed by -ing forms, and which  are followed by infinitives. It is best to Unfortunately, there is no easy way to decide which  verbs, adjectives and nouns - the need to talk not She's good at listening. (     She's good to listen.) - She's ready to listen.

He suggested waiting. (     He suggested to wait.) - He agreed to wait.

follow certain verbs, adjectives or nouns (►   Section 9). Compare: Infinitives and -ing forms are often used in similar ways. For instance, they can 95 infinitive       or  -ing    form?
